title: Albizia subrhombea
taxonomy: Albizia subrhombea is classified under the family Fabaceae. This species falls under the subfamily Mimosoideae. Albizia subrhombea is a member of the genus Albizia. It is a type of flowering plant native to the tropical regions of Africa. The classification of Albizia subrhombea reflects its evolutionary relationships with other members of the Fabaceae family.
uses: Albizia subrhombea has been reported to have several medicinal uses, primarily in traditional African medicine. The plant's roots and leaves are used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin infections. Its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties make it effective in soothing wounds and reducing pain. Additionally, Albizia subrhombea has been used to treat digestive issues and to improve overall health. Further research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of using Albizia subrhombea for medicinal purposes.

parts: Albizia subrhombea. The medicinal parts of Albizia subrhombea include the bark, leaves, and roots, which have been used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments. preparations: Albizia subrhombea is known to have various uses in traditional herbal medicine, with preparations often made from its roots, leaves, and seeds. intro: Albizia subrhombea, also known as the Indian siris tree, is a species of flowering plant in the family Fabaceae, native to tropical Asia and India. The health benefits of Albizia subrhombea include its use as an anti-inflammatory and antioxidant agent, which can help to reduce fever and alleviate symptoms of various diseases. The bioactive constituents of Albizia subrhombea include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made from Albizia subrhombea include decoctions and infusions, which are used to treat various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues.
